test.command: go list ./internal/... | grep -v 'github.com/yusefmosiah/cogent/internal/adapters/codex' | grep -v 'github.com/yusefmosiah/cogent/internal/mcpserver' | xargs env GOMAXPROCS=4 go test

blockingIssues.description: The new "integration" test never drives the live transport. Its body only builds requests and inspects GetServerForRequest/GetServerInstance; there is no handler.ServeHTTP or session.CallTool invocation, so the requested end-to-end proof for external and supervisor-marked traffic is still missing.

SessionManager.SetBroadcastFunc only updates servers that already exist. serve.go installs it before any sessions are materialized, so later-created sessions such as the "channel-send" server never inherit the hub broadcast function and SendChannelEvent falls back to stdout instead of reaching serve-mode websocket clients.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require live transport regression coverage for MCP/StreamableHTTP changes; helper-level provenance assertions are not enough when the production handler path is part of the contract.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both provenance fix reviews noted that the tests stopped at helper-level request/server inspection and did not drive session.CallTool or StreamableHTTP end to end.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit CreatedBy="service" labeling on true service-generated follow-on paths so ActorService provenance stays visible and easy to review.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The service-created-by fix passed only after the callsites were explicitly labeled, and the review history shows this is easy to miss without a stronger guidance note.
